note: Reverse-engineered from the real RSC payload of /compare/superhuman-vs-gmail. Every field name below was read verbatim from the payload. The 'ssg*' names in the payload (ssgComparison, ssgComparisonApps) are just the page query's alias for Static-Site-Generation; the underlying Payload collections are 'comparisons' and 'apps'.
howAComparisonPageIsBuilt: 1. Page query loads ONE `comparisons` row (the matchup) -> gives name, tldr, and the 2 apps via `comparisonApps`.
2. For each app it pulls that app's `categoryRecommendations` (the app's standing inside a category).
3. Each categoryRecommendation carries: recommendation(enum) + classification(enum) + bestFor + summary(Lexical) + the `category` (which lists its `categoryCriteria`) + a `review`.
4. The `review` holds `reviewCategoryCriterionRating[]` = the actual per-criterion 1-7 scores (the 7/7, 3/7 bars).
5. The page renders app A's ratings next to app B's ratings, criterion by criterion. The comparison row itself stores NO scores.

criteria.name: TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. Innovation, Speed & Productivity)

category_criteria._comment: Junction: which criteria belong to a category. Order = position in the docs[] array.

category_recommendations._comment: An app's editorial standing inside one category. This is the 'verdict' object.

reviews._comment: The detailed review attached to a category_recommendation; holds the per-criterion ratings.

review_category_criterion_ratings._comment: The actual 1-7 score for ONE criterion within ONE review. These are the bars on the page.

comparisons.name: TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. 'Superhuman Mail vs Gmail')

comparison_apps._comment: Junction: the (usually 2) apps in a matchup, with display order.

keyInsights: A comparison does NOT store scores. It only links 2 apps + a tldr. Scores live in review_category_criterion_ratings, reached via each app's category_recommendation -> review.
Scores are per (app, category, criterion) on a 1-7 scale, not per comparison. So the SAME review data powers every matchup that app appears in.
'Relevant / similar apps' are derived from shared category membership (category.appRecommendations), NOT a manual app-to-app mapping.
All long text (tldr, summary, per-criterion write-ups) is Lexical rich-text JSON, not plain strings.
